inline_comments.message:
If I see leader election I mean that this is how multiple instances of CMPv2 issuer are able to form the cluster and select the leader... 
Is such supported by CMPV2 external issuer? If so, this role may be required!

Yes, leader election is supported by CMPv2 issuer. Now it is disabled and can be enabled by setting "--enable-leader-election" in args section in issuer deployment.
I have checked it, when leader election was enabled and it works without these roles, which are defined here.

Hmmm in general we are now in progress of adding a dedicated service account to every component so...

Maybe instead of just removing you could start using the common template that we have for this?

In order to not block this change with removal kube-rbac-proxy I have reverted roles.yaml to the previous state in Patchset 2.

Moreover I have prepared the new MR with adding service account - please review https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/123268.

Unfortunately common template doesn't support ClusterRole, so it isn't possible to fully transform it to using template.
